zh.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne於大約1365年出生在愛爾蘭萊音斯特。她於大約1384年與King Ardgal MacMahon結婚。他們至少是12個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1409年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne于大约1365年出生在爱尔兰萊音斯特。她于大约1384年与King Ardgal MacMahon结婚。他们至少是12个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1409年在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Herne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Hearn
meanings: Irish (Waterford and Kilkenny): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEachthighearna ‘descendant of Eachthighearna’, a personal name meaning ‘lord of horses’, from each ‘horse’ + tighearna ‘master, lord’.

English: topographic name from Middle English herne, hirne, or hurne, from Old English hyrne ‘nook or corner of land’ (i.e. an out-of-the-way, secluded place) or ‘bend of a river’. The name may also be habitational, from Herne (Kent), Hurn (Dorset), or Hirn (Hampshire), with the same etymology as the topographic term. In post-medieval English pronunciation the three forms have converged and the spellings become interchangeable. Hurn may sometimes have been pronounced and spelled the same as the surname Horn .
English: habitational name from Herne in Tottington, Bedfordshire, so called from the dative plural (originally used after a preposition) of Old English hær ‘stone’, i.e. ‘at the stones’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne was born about 1365, in Leinster, Ireland. She married King Ardgal MacMahon about 1384. They were the parents of at least 12 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1409, in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne est née environ 1365, à Leinster, Irlande. Elle a épousé King Ardgal MacMahon environ 1384. Ils ont eu au moins 12 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1409, à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne wurde um 1365 in Leinster, Irland geboren. Sie hat um 1384 King Ardgal MacMahon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 12 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1409 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne è nata il circa 1365 nel Leinster, Irlanda. Lei sposato King Ardgal MacMahon circa 1384. Erano genitori di almeno 12 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1409, nel Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herneは約1365年にアイルランドレインスターで生まれた。約1384年にKing Ardgal MacMahonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも12人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1409年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーで45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne(은)는 대략 1365년 아일랜드 레인스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1384년 King Ardgal MacMahon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1409년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, Un: Tewkesbury, Glevum, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne nasceu aproximadamente 1365, em Leinster, Irlanda. Ela casou-se com King Ardgal MacMahon aproximadamente 1384. Eles tiveram pelo menos 12 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1409, em Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne родилась около 1365 г. в местности линстир, Ирландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени King Ardgal MacMahon около 1384 г. У них было по крайней мере 12 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1409 г. в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Queen Margaret de la Herne nació aproximadamente 1365, en Leinster, Irlanda. Se casó con King Ardgal MacMahon aproximadamente 1384. Fueron padres de por lo menos 12 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1409, en Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 45 años.
